mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and improves equity valuation, so sustained cuts should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling for 2 consecutive months, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above an already elevated level signals weakening household income and demand, which should pressure S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales accelerate while housing starts remain subdued, the mix implies consumption support without broad construction-led cyclicality; that configuration should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth is positive while HOUST is below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either single proxy alone in walk-forward tests, or if median 20-day SPX returns are non-positive under the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Improving industrial production indicates rising real activity and profit expectations, so positive monthly momentum in INDPRO should precede higher S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX direction is not positive in at least 53% of months with positive INDPRO momentum over the prior month, or if rank correlation is near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts is a leading sign of credit and demand weakness, so a monthly decline in HOUST should forecast negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% year over year
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if the trigger does not coincide with higher drawdown frequency than unconditional periods.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation stays contained while FEDFUNDS remains extremely low, the market interprets policy as supportive liquidity, which should push S&P 500 higher over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation over 3 months below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined low-rate low-inflation regime does not beat the unconditional mean return over 20 trading days, or if positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a severe unemployment regime, further increases in PAYEMS signal stabilization rather than overheating, and that labor improvement should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE stays above 8% while PAYEMS rises for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-repair regime fails to produce positive 60-day median SPX returns or if the signal reverses sign when unemployment remains above 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP combined with falling payrolls marks broad final-demand deterioration, so that combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if months with negative GDP growth and falling payrolls do not underperform the unconditional 20-day SPX return distribution, or if the conditional hit rate is not below 45%.
